g.text: Running Hash State H[0..7] (32 bytes = 256 bits)
uint32_t H[8] — persists across ALL blocks
MUST reset to these values before every new hash
Block N: H_N → compress(H, block_N) → H_final
H_final encodes ALL blocks — not just last block
Length extension: H_final IS the internal state
1. Append 0x80 byte after last message byte
Block i starts at data + i*64
All additions mod 2^32 (uint32_t wraps in C)
Output: uint32_t W[64] (256 bytes on stack)
ROTR(x,n): bits wrap | no bits lost | bijection
SHR(x,n): bits lost | irreversible | n zeros in
σ uses both: ROTR (diffuse) + SHR (destroy info)
Swapping these = wrong hash, no error signal
Step 2: Init Working Vars from H[0..7]
T1 = h + Σ1(e) + Ch(e,f,g) + K[t] + W[t]
Ch(x,y,z) = (x&y)^(~x&z) — MUX: x chooses y or z
H is now updated — input for next block
Ch, Maj: AND creates GF(2) cross-terms xy
Mod addition: carry bits couple all bit positions
Total: ~112 bytes — no heap alloc needed
Invariant: buf_len in [0,63] after every update()
v3 tests: boundary case + Merkle-Damgard chain
If v1+v2 pass but v3 fails: check num_blocks==2
